data.title: Wall Street ends mixed, Nasdaq survives from selloff amid valuation worries
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages closed mixed, with the Nasdaq Composite ((COMP:IND)) scrawling up, although ending the week with loses after investors sold off over worries about lofty valuations.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) closed -0.1%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) ended +0.1%, and the Dow (DJI) finished -0.7%.

Both the S&P 500 ((SP500)) and the Dow ((DJI)) added to weekly gains -- +0.1% and +0.3%, respectively -- while the Nasdaq ((COMP:IND)) ended the week -0.5%.

“Investors’ experience of the market this week was determined by whether they owned high-beta growth names or a more regular collection of workaday equities, bonds, ETFs and whatnot. The grownups experience of this week has been ‘meh, a garden variety spot of weakness’; basement-dwelling degenerates everywhere are thinking about how to explain to their Moms that they are going to need more allowance next month, on account of what has happened,” Seeking Alpha analyst Alex King of Cestrian Capital Research said.

“Everyone has to learn this lesson once; if you were burned in 2021 you probably wised up already. If this is your first rodeo, congratulations, you have now paid tuition fees.  We continue to look higher into year-end,” King added.

Four out of the seven “Magnificent” stocks ended in the red, including Alphabet (GOOG) -0.9%, Amazon (AMZN) -1.3%, Apple (AAPL) -0.3%, and Meta (META) -0.1%.

Financials (XLF) was one of the sectors lagging the most, with big bank names such as Goldman Sachs (GS), American Express (AXP), Visa (V), and JPMorgan Chase (JPM) among the top decliners within the Dow ((DJI)).

Adding to investors' worries, odds of a December 25-basis-point interest rate cut fell to 45.9%, according to the CME FedWatch tool.

Also, economic releases that had been delayed due to the U.S. government shutdown could start to trickle down next week, giving investors and the Fed a lot to ponder on before the December monetary policy meeting.

On crypto, Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is now below $95,000, reaching bear market territory.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.15%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 3.62%.

Wall Street's major indexes closed significantly lower on Thursday as heightened market sentiment due to the end of the U.S. government shutdown dissipated, and focus was turned to the economy.

More on markets:

data.title: Quantum Computing GAAP EPS of $0.01 beats by $0.07, revenue of $0.38M beats by $0.26M
data.description:
• Quantum Computing press release (QUBT): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.01 beats by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $0.38M (+280.0% Y/Y) beats by $0.26M.
  • Shares +4.93%.

  • Dr. Yuping Huang, Interim Chief Executive Officer of QCi, commented, "The third quarter marked another important step forward for QCi as we strengthened our balance sheet, deepened commercial and government relationships, and advanced our roadmap toward scalable quantum and photonic manufacturing. Revenues increased 280% year-over-year for the three months ended September 30, 2025, reflecting our sales progress and increasing demand for our products, including a recent sale to a top 5 U.S. bank.

    "We ended the third quarter with $352 million in cash and $461 million in investments, and subsequent to the quarter raised an additional $750 million, giving us a substantial liquid position of over $1.5 billion today to execute our long term growth strategy. Our integrated photonic approach continues to differentiate QCi as we work to bring practical quantum technologies out of the lab and into the hands of people. We remain focused on technical execution, strategic hiring, and thoughtful scaling of our foundry and quantum systems to meet growing global demand."

data.title: Occidental extends winning streak, shares rise for seventh consecutive session
data.description:
Occidental Petroleum (OXY) rose 1.75% to close at $42.76 on Friday, marking the seventh consecutive session of gains. The stock has moved higher through the past week, including a 2.51% rise on November 7 and a 1.38% increase on November 6.

The Texas-based oil & gas firm posted mixed third-quarter results earlier this week, with profit coming in better than expected while revenue lagged slightly. The company said it plans to keep production steady next year and lower spending, and noted softer performance in its midstream and marketing business. Occidental has also been in focus after agreeing to sell its OxyChem unit to Berkshire Hathaway for $9.7B, a move aimed at cutting debt and improving cash flow.

An analyst said Occidental has room for long-term upside as the company cuts costs, improves breakeven levels and sees new potential in the Barnett Shale. The analyst said the chemicals sale should help lower debt and support the company’s plans. Adding to this, the analyst wrote, “This was probably one of the better operational quarters reported.”

Looking at Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating, it has a “hold" recommendation, scoring 2.96 out of 5. The company earned an A+ in profitability, while a D+ in valuation and growth, a D in momentum and C- in revisions, dragged the overall rating.

While Seeking Alpha analysts are bullish, rating the stock Buy.

On Wall Street, analysts also lean cautious. OXY is rated “Hold”, with seven out of 25 analysts rating the stock Buy or higher, 15 recommending a Hold, while three analysts have a Sell recommendation.

Last month, ScotiaBank upgraded Occidental to Buy from Hold, lifting the price target to $55 from $48. Said, the proposed $9.7 billion OxyChem sale would speed up debt retirement and pull forward share buybacks, while still supporting the dividend.

Recently, OXY has declared a $0.24 per-share quarterly dividend, payable on January 15 to shareholders of record on December 10.

Year to date, the stock is down -13.8%.

data.title: U.S. rig count rises for fourth time in five weeks, Baker Hughes says
data.description:
The number of oil and gas drilling rigs in the U.S. gained 1 to 549, enough for a fourth gain in five weeks but stlll down 35 rigs, or 6%, from last year at this time, Baker Hughes said Friday in its latest weekly report.

Drilling rigs targeting crude oil in the U.S. rose by 3 to 417, but gas rigs gave back 3 to 125, while 7 rigs were classified as miscellaneous, in the week ending November 14.

Rigs targeting oil in the Permian Basin gained 3 to 253, the Eagle Ford lost 1 to 28, and the Williston Basin also lost 1 to 29.

ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Activist investor Irenic took stake in Papa John's in Q3 - report
data.description:
Activist investor Irenic Capital took a stake in Papa John's during the third quarter. Papa John's rose 2%.

It wasn't immediately clear what Irenic's intentions are for Papa John's, according to a Reuters report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter. The size of Irenic's stake wasn't known.

Irenic declined to comment to Reuters, while Papa John's did not respond to a request for comment. 

Irenic's stake is likely to be made public when the investor files its 13F filing on Friday, the deadline for the quarterly submission to be made.

The latest update comes after Reuters reported last Tuesday that Apollo Global (APO) withdrew a $64/share takeover offer for the pizza chain.

data.title: Activist investor builds stake in Papa John's - Reuters
data.description:
• Activist investor Irenic Capital Management has accumulated a stake in pizza chain Papa John’s (PZZA), according to sources cited by Reuters.
  • The size of Irenic’s stake and its intentions for the company are currently unknown.
  • According to the company's website, Irenic "invests in change." 
  • The disclosure briefly drove Papa John’s (PZZA) shares into the green for the day before retreating.   
  • Papa John’s (PZZA) has recently been the target of takeover speculation with the latest suitor, Apollo Capital Management (APO), pulling its $64 per share offer.

data.title: Sempra raised to Buy at Goldman as increased focus on Texas drives earnings upside
data.description:
Sempra (SRE) edges higher in Friday's trading as Goldman Sachs upgraded the utility holding company to Buy from Neutral with a $106 price target, raised from $88, saying the company's increased focus on Texas should drive earnings upside in the medium term.

After lowering Sempra's (SRE) rating in February, Goldman analyst Carly Davenport said the company has seen improved management communication and execution, legislative wins including the approval of the unified tracker mechanism in Texas and the mitigation of California wildfire risk via SB 254, progress on the balance sheet through announced asset sales, and further growth in Texas, with more capex towards transmission and potential for a constructive rate case outcome in the coming quarters.

Davenport said Sempra’s (SRE) Texas utility Oncor is poised to benefit from increased data center load growth in the region, as well as higher business, infrastructure, and population growth in Texas, enabled by a constructive regulatory backdrop, with the preliminary capital
plan update at Oncor as highly supportive of upside potential, as management expects to boost capital investments by at least 30% in the Texas subsidiary, with further upside potential.

After reflecting the increased capital plan in Texas, Davenport raised Goldman's outlook for Oncor EPS growth to ~21% in 2025-29 from 15% previously, with the consolidated company’s EPS growth now at 10%-plus over the same period, compared to ~9.5% previously.

data.title: Flowserve downgraded at BofA as near-term nuclear upside now priced in
data.description:
Flowserve (FLS) -1% in Friday's trading as Bank of America downgraded shares to Neutral from Buy with a $73 price target, believing the near-term nuclear upside is priced into the shares, and weakness in the company's core end markets in oil/gas and chemicals likely extends into 2026.

60% of Flowserve's (FLS) sales are related to oil/gas and chemical markets, and key leading
indicators for these end markets continue to soften due to oil price weakness, including continued weak rig counts in 2025, according to BofA analysts led by Andrew Obin, adding that the company's organic sales lag U.S. rig counts by about five quarters.

Flowserve's (FLS) margin expansion is strong and likely will drive above-peer EPS growth in 2026, but the incrementals are largely priced in, Obin and his team also said.

While Flowserve (FLS) clearly will be a longer-term beneficiary from the nuclear opportunity, which is accretive to overall margins, BofA noted projects are long-cycle - booked out 2-3 years in advance - and bookings are lumpy.

data.title: Meta plans to assess employees on AI-driven performance next year: report
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) plans to begin including how its employees utilize artificial intelligence to improve their performance when it conducts annual reviews, starting next year, according to Business Insider.

According to the report, Meta's head of people, Janelle Gale, issued an internal memo on Thursday that said employees' usage of AI to improve productivity will become a "core expectation."

"As we move toward an AI-native future, we want to recognize people who are helping us get there faster," Gale wrote.

"It's well-known that this is a priority, and we're focused on using AI to help employees with their day-to-day work," said a Meta spokesperson, according to the report.

The "move toward an AI-native" future is similar to directives issued to employees across the tech world, including Amazon (AMZN), Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) and Microsoft (MSFT). 

The Washington Post reported back in June that companies such as Duolingo (DUOL), Shopify (SHOP) and Box (BOX) were already requiring employees to adopt AI tools for handling day-to-day assignments.

data.title: Crypto stocks see mixed response from short sellers in October
data.description:
Crypto stocks saw mixed interest from short sellers in the month of October. While four companies saw short interest decrease, the remaining four saw it increase in the month.

Bitfarms (BITF) saw the highest jump in short interest this month to 17.31% from 8.79% last month. Short interest in Strategy (MSTR), Coinbase (COIN) and Core Scientific (CORZ) increased to 9.74%, 6.49% and 19.87% respectively.

On the other hand, short interest in MARA Holdings (MARA), Riot Platforms (RIOT) and CleanSpark (CLSK) declined to 25.59%, 14.94%, 19.89% and 5.49%.

The short dollar volume in the crypto stocks stood at $16.93 billion in October. Of these, Strategy and Coinbase attracted the most interest from short sellers, accounting for $11.43 billion of the total bets.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) prices remained above the $100K mark throughout October. However, in November, the cryptocurrency plunged in tandem with the broader markets, falling below the $100K level. On November 13, bitcoin closed 1.91% lower at $99,765.06, its lowest since May 7.
